title: The One With The Jellyfish

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr.
lines.text: I’m your mother.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ehh?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, and then Monica joked that she wouldn’t go out with a guy like Chandler...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hi there.
lines.stage_direction: to Monica in a funny voice Monica turns her head away in disgust

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: ...and he couldn’t let it go, and... I don’t really know what happened with that either.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh-oh! And then Ross’s new girlfriend, Bonnie, shows up and Rachel convinced her to save her head. And then Ross and Rachel kiss, and now Ross has to choose between Rachel and the bald girl and I don’t know what happened there either...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Y'know what, hold on, let me go get Chandler.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Rachel and Bonnie
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Bonnie
lines.text: Rachel was just helping me out. My head got all sunburned.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Awww.

lines.speaker: Bonnie
lines.text: Thanks a million.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, you’re welcome a million.

lines.speaker: Bonnie
lines.text: Okay, I’ll see you in our room.
lines.stage_direction: getting up and leaving to Ross

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.
lines.stage_direction: closes the door, and goes over and kisses Rachel.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I know.

scenes.scene_description: The beach house, it’s the same scene from the end of last year, with Ross in front of the two doors of Rachel’s and Bonnie’s rooms, trying to decide which door to choose. He finally chooses the one his right and goes in.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, I gotta go.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Whoa! What?! Why?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, I-I gotta go break up with Bonnie.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Here?! Now?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, yeah. I can’t-I can’t stay here all night, and if I go in there she’s-she’s gonna wanna... do stuff.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, can’t you tell her that you are not in the mood?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, she likes that. Yeah. Faking sleep doesn’t work either, I can’t tell you how many mornings I woke up with her...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Whoa-ho.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Whoa-oh, okay! Yeah, why am I telling you that?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don’t know.

scenes.scene_description: They both kiss again and fall onto the bed.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, yeah. It wasn’t every morning.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, making it worse!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay.

scenes.scene_description: they kiss again

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr.
lines.text: So I guess you’d like to know how it all happened.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I-I mean I, well I think I can figure it out. I guess y'know I was born, and everyone started lying their asses off!

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr.
lines.text: Noo! No! It wasn’t like that I... Remember how I told you how Lily, Frank, and I we were, we were close. Well, we were, we were very close.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: How close?

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr.
lines.text: Well, the-the three of us we were, kind of umm, a couple.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I don’t even know how that would work!

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr.
lines.text: Well, we were...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I’m not asking!

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr.
lines.text: Well, any how, some how I got pregnant, and, and I was scared. I was stupid and sellfish, and I was 18 years old. I mean, you remember what it’s like to be eighteen years old?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah. Let’s see, my had Mom killed herself, and my Dad had run off, and I was living in a Gremlin with a guy named Cindy who talked to his hand.

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr.
lines.text: Well, I’m so sorry. I thought I was leaving you with the best parents in the world, I didn’t even hear about your Mom and Dad til a couple of years ago, and by then you were already grown up. I don’t know, you’re here, and I would, I would really, I would like to get to know you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, well, everybody does! I’m a really cool person. And y'know you had 29 years to find that out, but you didn’t even try! Y'know what, you walked out on me, and I’m just, I’m gonna do the same thing to you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr.
lines.text: Wait!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I don’t ever want to see you again!

scenes.scene_description: Phoebe Sr.’s house, it’s right after she told Phoebe that she’s her birth mother.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Umm, where’s my purse?
lines.stage_direction: walking back in with her hand over her eyes.

scenes.scene_description: She walks out and slams the door.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Shoot! We’re out of soda.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, I’ll go out and get you some.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Really?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Nope! Because I’m not your boyfriend. Hey Pheebs, how did it go?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, umm, my Mom’s friend, Phoebe, is actually my birth Mom.

scenes.scene_description: The beach house, Joey and Chandler are sitting at the dinner table, Monica is looking in the fridge.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I found a dried up seashores.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Sweety, what are you talking about?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, my new Mom, who-who’s a big, fat abandoner!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Whoa, wait, Pheebs, wait a second! Don’t you wanna stay here and talk about it?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No. I’m just, I wanna, I need to be alone.

scenes.scene_description: The gang is shocked.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Monica!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh.

scenes.scene_description: She starts to go upstairs.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It’s over.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, was it awful?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, it was loong. I didn’t even realise how late it was, until I noticed the 5 o’clock shadow on her head. Anyway, she didn’t want to stay. I called a cab; she just left.
lines.stage_direction: They both start to laugh, then stop themselves quickly.

scenes.scene_description: The beach house, Rachel’s bedroom. She is finishing up writing something as Ross walks through the door.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I wrote you a letter.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ohh! Thank you! I like mail.
lines.stage_direction: He goes to kiss her again, but she turns away.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It’s just some things I’ve been thinking about. Some things about us, and before we can even think about the two of us getting back together, I just need to know how you feel about this stuff.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay. Wow, it’s-it’s 5:30 in the morning. So, I’d better get cracking on this baby.
lines.stage_direction: He leans in to kiss her again, but she leans back preventing him from making contact. Rachel laughs

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I’ll be waiting for you, just come up when you’re done.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, I’ll be up in, 18 pages. Front and back. Very exciting.

scenes.scene_description: They kiss.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh. Oh-oh.
lines.stage_direction: looks at his watch takes a drink of coffee and resumes reading the letter

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey! What happened to you? Why didn’t you come up?
lines.stage_direction: coming down the stairs Ross jumps up, and quickly puts the letter back together, pretending like he has just finished it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Done!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You just finished?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, I wanted to be thorough. I mean this-this is clearly very, very important to you, to us! And so I wanted to read every word carefully, twice!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So umm, does it?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I’m sorry.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Does it?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Does it? Does it? Yeah, I wanted to give that whole ‘Does it?’ part just another glance.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What are you talking about, Ross, you just said that you read it twice! Look, y'know what, either it does or it doesn’t, and if you have to even think about it...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, Rach, no. I don’t, I don’t, I don’t have to think about it, in fact, I’ve decided, I’ve decided that, that it.......does.

scenes.scene_description: The beach house, the next morning. Ross is passed out on the kitchen counter. He wakes up with a start and has one of the pages of the letter stuck to his face.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Are you sure?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, sure! I’m sure.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know.
lines.stage_direction: Hugs him more violently this time and pushes him back away from the letter.

scenes.scene_description: Rachel stands there for a moment, starting to cry. Then gasps and runs over and hugs him. While hugging her, Ross tries to find the ‘Does it?’ part in the letter.

scenes.location: The beach. Chandler and Monica are out getting some sun

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right, there’s a nuclear holocaust, I’m the last man on Earth. Would you go out with me?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ennnh.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I’ve got canned goods.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, you guys! Take a look at this! Check this baby out, dug me a hole!
lines.stage_direction: jumping up in a hole that he is digging, he is shoulder deep Chandler and Monica jump up and go over to the hole.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Excellent hole, Joe.

scenes.scene_description: The beach. Chandler and Monica are out getting some sun.

scenes.location: The beach. Chandler and Monica are out getting some sun

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh no! No!! My hole!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ow!! Ow!!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?! What?!! What is it?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Jellyfish sting! Oh, it hurts! It hurts!! It hurts!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, can we help?! You want us to take you back to the house?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It’s like two miles!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, and I’m a little tired from digging the hole.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh damn the jellyfish. Damn all the jellyfish!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We’ve got to do something!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, there’s really only one thing you can do.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?! What is it?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You’re gonna have to pee on it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?!! Gross!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Don’t blame me, I saw it on The Discovery Channel .

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know what, he’s right. There’s something like uh, ammonia in that, that like kills the pain.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well forget it! It doesn’t hurt that baaad !!!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: If you want some privacy you can use my hole.

scenes.scene_description: A wave crashes on the beach and partially fills up his hole.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I’m ready to get the hell out of here! Oh. Are you? Are you?!! Ohh! That’s so great!! Ooh, not for Bonnie. But for you, yay! Ohh.
lines.stage_direction: Sees Ross and Rachel cuddling on the couch. they nod ‘Yes.’ they nod ‘No.’

scenes.scene_description: The beach house, Phoebe is coming down the stairs all packed and ready to go.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: How was the beach?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Nothing, I don’t know.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What happened?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Nothing. I’m gonna take a shower.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Me too!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Me too.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, I’m gonna put this in the car.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ooh, I have to go pack. It really does?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It does. It really and truly does.

scenes.scene_description: The rest of the gang arrives with their heads down in shame.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It so does not !!!
lines.stage_direction: finding the part looks up in disgust

scenes.scene_description: Rachel kisses him, and goes upstairs. After she’s gone, Ross frantically tries to find and read the ‘Does it?’ part.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: She wants me to take responsibility for everything that went wrong in our relationship. I mean she goes on for five pages about, about how I was unfaithful to her! WE WERE ON A BREAK!!!!!
lines.stage_direction: Both Joey and Chandler shrug their shoulders as to say ”Well...” yelling

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh my God! If you say that one more time, I’m gonna break up with you!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Fine! Fine! But this break-up was not all my fault, and she, she says here, “If you accept full responsibility...” Full responsibility! “...I can begin to trust you again. Does that seem like something you can do. Does it?!!”
lines.stage_direction: reading from the letter to Chandler and Joey yells at Joey

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look, Ross, you have what you want, you’re back with Rachel. If you bring this up now you’re gonna wreck the best thing that even happened to you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, I know. I mean, no, you’re right. Yeah I guess I’ll let it go. But you-you understand how-how hard it is to forget about this.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Sure, it’s hard to forget! But that doesn’t mean you have to talk about it! A lot of things happened on that trip that we should never, ever talk about.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What the hell happened on that beach?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It’s between us and the sea, Ross!

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Ross is telling Joey and Chandler about the letter.
Ross laughs and has a ‘Come on...’ look on his face as he looks and Chandler who nods his head in agreement with Joey. Ross is stunned.

scenes.location: A hallway in an apartment building. Phoebe is knocks on a door and it opens

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hi, Ursula.
lines.stage_direction: to the person that answered the door

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, well umm, I know that we haven’t talked in a long time, but umm okay, our Mom is not our birth Mom. This-this other lady is our birth Mom.

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Right, okay, the one that lives in Montuak, umm-hmm.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You know her?!

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: No, I umm, I read about her in Mom’s suicide note.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: There-there was a suicide note?! Well, do you still have it?

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Hang on.
lines.stage_direction: disgusted She goes into her apartment and slams the door in Phoebe’s face.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I can’t believe you didn’t tell me there was a suicide note!

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Yeah. So how have you been doing?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I, umm, shut up!

scenes.scene_description: A hallway in an apartment building. Phoebe is knocks on a door and it opens.

scenes.location: A hallway in an apartment building. Phoebe is knocks on a door and it opens

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: “Good-bye Phoebe and Ursula. I’ll miss you. P.S. Your Mom lives in Montauk.” You just wrote this!

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Well, it’s pretty much the gist. Well, except for the poem. You read the poem, right?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Noooo!!

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: All right, hang on!
lines.stage_direction: She takes the note, goes back into her apartment and slams the door shut.

scenes.scene_description: Ursula opens the door and hands her the note.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Pass the cheese, please.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's, Chandler, Monica, and Joey are sitting at the table eating dinner and not talking. Ross and Rachel are outside cuddling on the balcony.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: My God, you can’t even look at me! Can you?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Nope.

scenes.scene_description: Joey hands her the cheese without looking at her.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey! Phoebe! We can talk to Phoebe!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No. I’m-I’m to depressed to talk.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I’ll give you a thousand dollars to talk to us.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, you guys! What do you, what do you think about making that beach trip an annual thing?

lines.speaker: Chandler, Monica, and Joey
lines.text: NO!!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right, that’s it, you guys! What happened out there?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What? We took a walk, nothing happened. I can back with nothing all over me.

lines.speaker: Ross and Rachel
lines.text: Come on!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What happened? Joey?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No! Joey, we swore we’d never tell!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: They’ll never understand!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, we have to say something! We have to get it out! It’s eating me alive!! Monica got stung by a jellyfish.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right!! All right. I got stung. Stung bad. I couldn’t stand. I-I couldn’t walk.
lines.stage_direction: interrupting walks slowly into the living room

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We were two miles from the house. Scared and alone. We didn’t think we could make it.
lines.stage_direction: following Monica He goes to put his hands on Monica’s shoulders but for some reason can quite complete the action and pulls back.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I was in too much pain.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And I was tired from digging the huge hole!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And then Joey remembered something.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I’d seen this thing on The Discovery Channel ...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wait a minute! I saw that! On The Discovery Channel , yeah! About jellyfish and how if you... Ewwww!! You peed on yourself?!
lines.stage_direction: stops suddenly and turns to look at Monica

lines.speaker: Phoebe and Rachel
lines.text: Ewwww!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You can’t say that!! You-you don’t know!! I mean I thought I was gonna pass out from the pain! Anyway I-I tried, but I-I couldn’t...bend that way. So...

lines.speaker: Phoebe, Ross, and Rachel
lines.text: Ewwww!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That’s right I stepped up! She’s my friend and she needed help! And if I had too, I’d pee on anyone of you! Only, uhh, I couldn’t. I got the stage fright. I wanted to help, but there was too much pressure. So-so I uh, I turned to Chandler.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Joey kept screaming at me, “Do it now! Do it!! Do it! Do it now!!” Sometimes late at night I can still here the screaming.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That’s ‘cause sometimes I just do it through my wall to freak you out.

scenes.scene_description: Phoebe enters.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: “...fuchsia and mauvvve. Those are the 66 colours of my bedroommmm.” Thank you, thank you. Ohh, and I invite you to count the colours in your bedroom. Except for you. You go away.
lines.stage_direction: singing applause Sees that Phoebe Sr. has entered, and to her

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr.
lines.text: I’ll go in a second, I-I just wanted to tell you that there hasn’t been a day where I didn’t regret giving you up.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, bye.

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr.
lines.text: No, I’m not done. I-I-I just want you to know that I, the reason I didn’t look you up was, well I was afraid that you’d react, just well like, the way, the way you’re reacting right now, and can’t we just, y'know, start from here?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr.
lines.text: Sorry. But just one last thing. Y'know you came looking for family. I’m family, I’m it. Now, now I’m done.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: But, it’s not like we’re losing anything. Y'know?

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr.
lines.text: Yeah, I guess you’re right.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It’s not like we-we know each other or anything. Or that have anything in common.

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr.
lines.text: Well, I don’t know. I mean it’s not like we don’t have anything in common. I mean I like uh, pizza.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I-I like pizza!

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr.
lines.text: You do?! Wait, I like umm, the Beetles.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God, so do I!

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr.
lines.text: I knew it, wow!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wait-wait-wait, wait! Puppies. Cute or ugly?

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr.
lines.text: Ohh, so cute.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh-huh, well! But umm, still I’m-I’m mad at you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr.
lines.text: I know. I’m mad at me too.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well umm, do you wanna get something to eat? I’m kinda hungry.

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr.
lines.text: Hey! Me too!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right, stop it. Now you’re just doing it to freak me out.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Phoebe is finishing up a song.

scenes.location: Rachel’s bedroom. Ross and Rachel have just finished consummating the new relationship

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh-hooo, I missed you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I missed you too.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ooh, I was soo nervous about that letter. But the way you owned up to everything, it just showed me how much you’ve grown. Y'know?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I suppose.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You have! Ross, you should give yourself credit. I mean my Mom never thought this would work out. It was all, “Once a cheater, always a cheater.”

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Umm-hmm.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ooh, I just wish we hadn’t lost those four months, but if time was what you needed just to gain a little perspective...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: WE WERE ON A BREAK!!!!!!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Coffee house?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You bet.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And for the record, it took two people to break up this relationship!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah! You and that girl from that copy place, which yesterday you took full responsibility for!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I didn’t know what I was taking full responsibility for! Okay?! I didn’t finish the whole letter!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I fell asleep!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You fell asleep?!

lines.speaker: It was 5
lines.text: 30 in the morning, and you had rambled on for 18 pages. Front and back!! Oh-oh-oh, and by the way, Y-O-U-apostrophe-R-E means ‘you are,’ Y-O-U-R means ‘your!’
lines.stage_direction: they go into the living room, trapping Monica, Chandler, and Joey in the kitchen to Rachel

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Y'know I can’t believe I even thought about getting back together again! We are so over!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: FINE BY ME!!
lines.stage_direction: starts to cry he opens the door and traps Chandler behind it

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, oh, and hey-hey-hey, those little spelling tips will come in handy when you’re at home on Saturday nights playing Scrabble with Monica!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Sorry!! I just feel bad about all that sleep you’re gonna miss wishing you were with me!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, no-no-no don’t you worry about me falling asleep. I still have your letter!!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And hey! Just so you know, it’s not that common! It doesn’t happen to every guy! And it is a big deal!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I KNEW IT!!!!

scenes.scene_description: Rachel’s bedroom. Ross and Rachel have just finished consummating the new relationship.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Gin.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We were playing Gin? Y'know if we were a couple, we could play this game naked.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Will you stop!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay. All right.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, all right, I think you’re great, I think you’re sweet, and you’re smart, and I love you. But you will always be the guy who peed on me.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's, Chandler and Monica are playing cards at the dinner table.

title: The One With The Cat

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wow! That ripped! That ripped real nice!

lines.speaker: Joey

My Mother’s favourite fish is Orange Roughy... Cats....like....fish! Hi, Mommy. Oh, I haven’t seen this smile in 17 years!

As we walks through the door, and without looking up, he goes to throw his keys on the foosball table, only, it’s gone and the keys it the floor. He then looks up at an empty apartment; everything is gone except for the entertainment center and that ceramic dog. Even the food in the fridge!

lines.text: I know!

lines.text: No! No! Look—Hey, enough is enough! Look, I am sorry that you feel guilty or whatever about spending time with your new mom, but this is not your old mom. This is a cat! Okay, Julio the cat! Not mom! Cat!

lines.text: Okay, then you don’t know what it feels like when one of them comes back. Do you? I believe this is my Mother. Even if I’m wrong, who cares? Just be a friend. Okay? Be supportive.

lines.text: Okay.

lines.text: Phoebe, his music could not get any worse. There are rats in the basement that are hanging themselves.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Ross is playing his music. Monica, Phoebe, and Rachel are sitting on the couch.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thank you, thanks. Yeah, I lost it. Y’know, I’m not gonna play anymore, would you, can you finish my set?
lines.stage_direction: Sits down next to the girls to Phoebe

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: After that? Yeah! No, I mean if I can help.

scenes.scene_description: Ross finally finishes with the same crash, and gets some applause.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, like I could lose it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I played bad on purpose guys.

scenes.scene_description: Phoebe gets up and goes to play, Ross goes over and sits down next to Monica and Rachel.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, so you were trying to play bad this whole time.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah—no, just that last song.

scenes.scene_description: Both Monica and Rachel laugh.

title: The One With Chandler In A Box

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, it’s me. I know you can’t stand to be in the same room as me, so I just thought I’d try and apologize over the phone. All I…

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Joey's, Joey is sitting at the counter reading a magazine as the phone rings.
Joey hangs up the phone in disgust.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look I never should have kissed your girlfriend, but I’m…

scenes.scene_description: The phone rings again.
Joey hangs up the phone again.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Stop callin’!!

lines.speaker: Voice
lines.text: Hey! Hey! Hey! This is 92.3, WXRK, K-Rock for our $1,000 daily challenge.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right!

lines.speaker: Voice
lines.text: What is the name of your roommate who is very, very sorry and would do anything…
lines.stage_direction: Joey realizes it’s Chandler and hangs up the phone in anger.

scenes.scene_description: The phone rings yet again.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Aww, turkey! Aww, giving thanks! Aww!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Look everyone, it’s the spirit of Thanksgiving!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So are things with you and Joey any better?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: They couldn’t be worse. I spent eight hours calling him last night, just trying to get him to talk to me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh wow, eight hours? So you could probably really use one of those plug-in telephone headsets huh?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Should we all expect Christmas gifts that can be stolen from your office?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You shouldn’t.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Speaking of Christmas, umm since Monica and I are starting a new business and have like no money, umm, this year maybe we could do secret Santa, and then we each only buy one gift. And-and there’s the added mystery of who gets who.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Who gets whom. I don’t know why I do that.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I’m gonna take a nap, turkey makes me sleepy.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We haven’t eaten yet!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know, but all that work you’re doing to get it ready, I just…

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, by any chance did either of pick uh Rachel for your secret Santa, ‘cause I wanna trade for her.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I picked her! Oh thank God you want her! Ooh!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wow! Why do you want to get rid of her so badly?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Because she exchanges every gift she ever gets, it’s like impossible to get her something she likes. Come on, let’s trade!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh that’s not true! That’s not true! I got her that backpack and she loved it! I remember how much she was crying the day when that big dog ran off with it… Oh, there was no big dog. All right this sucks! I already got her this briefcase, and I had R.G. put on it… Her initials…
lines.stage_direction: notices the look on Monica and Phoebe’s faces. Phoebe looks confused.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ohh.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, maybe you could give to somebody else. Ooh, like Ross Geller.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Op, y'know what though, it’s kind’ve a girlie briefcase.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Who cares? He works in a museum!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, what time is it? The big game is about to start!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You don’t have to do that, Ross and Joey aren’t here, you can watch the parade if you want.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What is wrong with this freezer?! Ow! Ow!!
lines.stage_direction: She jabs her arm into the freezer and a piece of ice flies into her eye.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: God, what happened?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God, ice just got in my eye!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: People are trying to sleep in here!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Monica got ice in her eye, and it hurts.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Open it up, let me see.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, y’know what, I can’t, it really kills.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well maybe you should put some ice on it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh, God it looks bad.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Honey, maybe we should take you to a doctor.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, my eye doctor is Richard! I can’t go to him when I don’t have a boyfriend!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: He’s really picky about his patients.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Honey, you’ve got to go. What’s his office number?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Like I remember his office number! Speed dial 7.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hi! Yeah, I’m calling on behalf of Monica Geller’s eye, and is um, is Richard Burke in today. He’s out of town, but does she want to see the on-call doctor?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes! She’s very excited about that.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: This is so cool, ‘til Monica gets back, it’s like I’m head chef and I get to make all the decisions.
lines.stage_direction: She looks at the remaining butter, and then decides to add it to the dish.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey-hey, I thought she told you to follow the recipe exactly!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, get out of my kitchen!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right! Okay! I think I am making some progress with Joey, when I went into the apartment he went straight into his bedroom but he only slammed the door once! I mean yeah, he gave me the finger while doing it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right I… I gotta call my mom and ask her a left handed cooking question.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So listen ah, I picked Monica for secret Santa, but I’m already getting her something for Chanukah, I was wondering if you wanna switch.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh y’know what, I was already trying to trade for ah, well, you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Really?! Wow! That’s-that’s so nice, what are you gonna get me?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don’t know R.G., I was thinking something girlie for your office.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, well maybe Phoebe will switch with me.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh no-no-no, you don’t want to do that, then you’re gonna get stuck with Rachel and she exchanges every gift she ever gets.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, that’s not true! I’ve got her lots of stuff she never took back.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Like?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Like uh, that gold necklace I got her last year.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: When was the last time you saw her wear it?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, she wore it all Christmas day, and then uh…

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Big dog?

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's, everyone but Joey and Chandler are there getting ready for Thanksgiving.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I’m sending back all this stuff that Chandler bought out of guilt.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Everything? Even the TV?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No! I’m putting that in my room.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Listen, Joey, I know what he did was wrong but don’t you think you could at least hear the guy out?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Back when you and Rachel were together, if Chandler had kissed her, would you hear him out?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That’s a good point. So uh, how long are you gonna punish him?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Five years.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You’ve sentenced him?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! Don’t do the crime if you can’t do the time.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Joey, the guy’s your best friend.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, was my best friend. Anyway, I don’t know why you’re pushing for him so hard. With him out of the way as my best friend, there’s a spot open.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, who? Me?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow! I’m honored! And y’know what I’m gonna do as my first act as your best friend?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I’m gonna get you to talk to Chandler.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right. But if you weren’t my best friend.

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Joey's, Joey is repacking the furniture into boxes to return it.

lines.speaker: Kathy
lines.text: Oh my God, is it really that bad?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I walk into a room and he won’t even talk to me, he just mumbles something in Italian. And I know he only knows the bad words.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey Gunther, have you uh, have you seen Chandler?

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: I thought you were Chandler. But umm, one of who is over there.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Chandler and Kathy are sitting at a table and talking about Joey.

lines.speaker: Kathy
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey Joe.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (Something in Italian. Storms out.)

scenes.scene_description: Joey turns around and sees them kissing.

lines.speaker: Doctor
lines.text: I’ll be right with you. Okay? Thanks, Wendy.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God! How cute is the on-call doctor?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ooh, so cute, that I’m thinking about jamming this pen in my eye.

scenes.scene_description: Dr. Burke’s office, Rachel and Monica are waiting as the doctor arrives.

lines.speaker: Nurse
lines.text: Dr. Burke will see you know.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh no-no-no, not Dr. Burke. Dr. Burke is out of town. The-the on-call doctor will see me now.

lines.speaker: Nurse
lines.text: Dr. Richard Burke is out of town. Dr. Timothy Burke, his son, will see you now.

lines.speaker: Dr. Timothy Burke
lines.text: Ready?

scenes.scene_description: The intercom buzzes.
Monica looks at Rachel, who gives her the thumbs up.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey! I’m sorry! That— where are you going?
lines.stage_direction: sees that Joey is about to leave

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: My folks.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, uh, when-when are you coming back?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don’t know. I might stay there for a few days while I look for an apartment.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, y’know at first I thought we could talk about this y’know, work it out, but uh, seeing you two together I don’t think I…

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, look, what do you want me to say? Do you want me to say that I’ll stop seeing her?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look, it’s not about her. Okay? But seeing you two together just reminds me of what you did. And I don’t want to live with some one who doesn’t know what it is to be a friend. So, I’ll see ya.
lines.stage_direction: He starts to leave, but Chandler grabs his bag and stops him.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, look, I know what it is to be a friend, I just-I just screwed up!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah! You did! And that’s why I’m leaving.

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Joey's, Joey is packing as Chandler rushes in.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right look, if you’re not gonna stay for me, then at least stay for them! Okay, they have had a very difficult year! What with the robbery and all!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: When that guy was robbing us, and I was locked in the entertainment unit for like six hours, you know what I was doing in there all that time? I was thinking about how I let you down!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah! But if would’ve know what kind of friend you were gonna turn out to be, I wouldn’t have worried about it so much! See you around!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right, wait! Come on! Just wait one second! There has to be something that I can do! Something! If we still had that entertainment unit I would get in it for six hours and think about how I let you down. What?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: We’ve got a box.
lines.stage_direction: Motions to a large wooden box next to him.

scenes.scene_description: The chick and the duck walk into the living room.

lines.speaker: Tim
lines.text: Last time I saw you, it was the morning I left for college. And you were just standing outside The Dairy Queen .

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I was probably waiting for it to open.

lines.speaker: Tim
lines.text: I gotta tell you, you look great now.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You look great too.

lines.speaker: Tim
lines.text: You’re an excellent patient!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So how does it look?

lines.speaker: Tim
lines.text: Well, you’ve got a little scratch on your cornea, your gonna have to wear a patch for a couple of days.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Like a pirate?!

lines.speaker: Tim
lines.text: If that helps you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Umm, so how long have you been working with your dad? Come on, one of us had to mention him.

lines.speaker: Tim
lines.text: I moved back here a couple of months ago.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh. Big family dinner tonight?

lines.speaker: Tim
lines.text: Uh.
lines.stage_direction: He holds up a brown lunch bag.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No way!

lines.speaker: Tim
lines.text: I was gonna have Thanksgiving at my girlfriend’s.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Tim
lines.text: But we broke up.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Tim
lines.text: She-she wasn’t ready for a serious commitment.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh. So you made her walk the plank? Aye, matie?

lines.speaker: Tim
lines.text: You’re not wearing the patch yet.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know.

scenes.scene_description: Dr. Burke’s office, Tim is examining Monica.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: He’s coming here for Thanksgiving!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know, it’s sick.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Why is it sick?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Because it’s Richard’s son! It’s like inviting Greek tragedy over for dinner!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, come on, Phoebe, you understand don’t you?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I can see where I’d be your best shot but, no. I’m sorry, but I think it’s twisted.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What’s twisted?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Me going out with Richard’s son.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ewwwww!! Ew! Ew! Ew!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Sounds like a really bad idea to me.
lines.stage_direction: He is now in the box, in their living room.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Is he okay in there?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: He’s fine!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, y'know, Mon, if things wrong out between you and Richard’s son, you’d be able to tell your kids, that you slept with their grandfather.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Fine! Judge all you want to but, married a lesbian, left a man at the altar, fell in love with a gay ice dancer, threw a girl’s wooden leg in a fire, livin’ in a box!!
lines.stage_direction: points to Ross points to Rachel points to Phoebe points to Joey points to Chandler goes to her room

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So now, what exactly is the point of the box?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Chandler?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: The meaning of the box is three fold. One , it gives me the time to think about what I did. Two , it proves how much I care about my friendship with Joey. And three , it hurts!
lines.stage_direction: holds a finger up through the air hole holds up another finger holds up a third finger

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh hey! Hey uh, you remember the necklace I gave you last year? Can I see it?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I just wanna check something.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Now, we’ll see.
lines.stage_direction: He puts his hand over the hole on the box. to Chandler

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Air hole! Air hole!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Here it is! I love it. I wear it all the time.
lines.stage_direction: coming out of her bedroom with a necklace

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: The necklace I got you was gold, this one is silver.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Huh, well maybe it uh, it changed.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh my God! You actually exchanged it!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well isn’t it better that I exchanged it for something that I enjoy and that I can get a lot of use out of?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What did you get?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Credit.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's, It’s closer to dinner. Monica has just told everyone that Tim is coming to dinner.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I’ll get it! I’ll get it! I’ll get it! I’ll get it! I’ll get it! I’ll get it! I’ll get it! I’ll get it! I’ll get it! I’ll get it! I’ll get it! I’ll get it! I’ll get it! I’ll get it! Hi, Tim.
lines.stage_direction: running in from her bedroom opens the door

lines.speaker: Tim
lines.text: Hi.
lines.stage_direction: gives her a bottle of wine

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Thank you. Come meet my friends. This is uh, Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Tim
lines.text: Hi, nice to meet you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: And Joey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Tim
lines.text: Hi, Joey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: My brother, Ross.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: And Rachel. And that’s Chandler.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: How do ya do.

lines.speaker: Tim
lines.text: What’s…

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Umm, well, he’s…

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: He’s doin’ some thinkin’!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What happened? What happened?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You kissed my girlfriend!

scenes.scene_description: There’s a knock on the door.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ooh, I like those sunglasses.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Like ‘em, like ‘em? Or, I’d like to get store credit for that amount like ‘em?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (Swears in Italian, it’s the same term used by Joey earlier and Joey nods his approval.)
lines.stage_direction: Swears in Italian, it’s the same term used by Joey earlier and Joey nods his approval.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right everybody, this turkey is ready!

lines.speaker: Tim
lines.text: Where can I wash up?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Here, let me show you. Okay, the towels are hanging next to the sink, and umm, you can use the fancy soap.

lines.speaker: Tim
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Fancy soap? I thought we were savin’ that for the Pope!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: See he’s nice. Right?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, but Monica, do you actually want to be in a relationship where you can actually use the phrase, "That’s not how your dad used to do it."

lines.speaker: Tim
lines.text: Wow! Everything looks great! Where should I sit?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I saved you a seat.
lines.stage_direction: Motions to the one next to her.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Sick-sick-sick-sick.

scenes.scene_description: A commercial for sunglasses comes on.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I’ll get it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Gotcha!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That doesn’t sound like thinking to me!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Sorry!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Y’know I don’t think you should be talking at all in there! I think you’ve got to much thinking to do to be talking and making jokes!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, okay, you got it!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, can you pass me the yams?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Sure! Oh, and Joey’s got the mashed potatoes if you want to exchange them.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Would you stop?! What is the matter with you?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh-ho-ho, we’ve got company.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: There’s nothing the matter with me. See, I’m not completely devoid of sentiment, see I have feelings.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, fine.
lines.stage_direction: She gets up and walks into her bedroom

scenes.scene_description: There’s a loud knocking.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You can’t tell, but I’m trying to break the tension by mooning you guys!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, look! If this is just a big joke to you, then forget about it, all right?! This means something to me! And if it doesn’t mean anything to you, then you should get out of there, otherwise you’re just an idiot in a box!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You’re right, and I’m sorry! This means a lot to me! I want you to be my friend again! I swear, I won’t say another word tonight.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So are you gonna start taking this thing seriously?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Absolutely!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That sounds like another word to me! Are you gonna take this seriously? Okay.

scenes.scene_description: an awkward silence follows

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Don’t say that I have no sentiment! This is a movie stub from our first date! This is an eggshell from the first time you made me breakfast in bed! This is from the museum from the first time we… were together. Okay, maybe I exchange gifts sometimes, but I keep the things that matter!
lines.stage_direction: Starts to show Ross what’s in the box. Holds up a bone

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don’t know what to say, I’m sorry. Though, you’re not supposed to take these. It’s like a million years old, we, we actually, we had people looking for that.

scenes.scene_description: Rachel comes back carrying a shoe box.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ooh, this always happens.
lines.stage_direction: putting off her coat Her coat gets stuck.

lines.speaker: Tim
lines.text: Here, let me help.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Tim
lines.text: You-you have a very beautiful… eye.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Y’know all my friends think this is weird.

lines.speaker: Tim
lines.text: Y’know I-I thought it was gonna be weird, I mean I almost called and canceled, but it really isn’t.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know! I mean it’s like me and your dad, that’s a totally separate thing.

lines.speaker: Tim
lines.text: Oh, I totally agree.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We’re just two people who find each other very attractive. Right?

scenes.scene_description: Rachel glares at him.

lines.speaker: Tim
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Nothing. Nothing.

lines.speaker: Tim
lines.text: No-no really, was-was that not okay?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No-no-no that was good, it was, that was uh, that was a goood kiss…

lines.speaker: Tim
lines.text: Oh my God! It didn’t remind you of…

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Don’t say it!

lines.speaker: Tim
lines.text: No, but it did! Didn’t it?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Tim
lines.text: Oh man!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know!

scenes.scene_description: Tim leans in to kiss her. They stop, and when he tries to kiss her again, Monica pulls away.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I’ll get it.

lines.speaker: Kathy
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Kathy

Good-bye, Chandler.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Joey, Ross, and Chandler are making a pit stop on their party tour.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ohh, here you are. Y’know, I’m-I’m glad you decided to hear me out.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, I’m hearing.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I’ve been doing a lot of thinking. A lot! And umm, well, I came up with a whole bunch of businesses you can do with your van. Okay umm, you could be flower delivery person.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Or! A bakery delivery person.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I wa-I wa-I wa…

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Pizza?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Monica!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, I’ve got a whole bunch of uh-uh, stuff in this area, but umm, I’m getting the feeling that you don’t want to deliver.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay. I’m guessing that if you don’t want to deliver, you probably don’t want to pick stuff up either.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Y’know what, let’s do the catering business.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Really?! Are you sure?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, y'know I-I made a commitment to you. Y’know what, it’d be, it’d be fun.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh! It will be fun! Ohh! Yay! Oh! Okay, ooh, let’s plan the wedding reception. Wow! You really wanted me to do something with this van. Y’know what, I want you to take the chef job.
lines.stage_direction: She grabs the notebook which Monica used for her ideas and starts flipping page after page after page after page after page to find a blank one. pause

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Really?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah. That’s what you really want. Yeah, I don’t want to be the reason you’re unhappy, that would just make me unhappy, and I really don’t want to be the reason I’m unhappy.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Besides, it might be kinda fun to form the new A-Team.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, the next day, Phoebe is there.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, hi Mrs. Lynch! Is Joanna in already?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Lynch
lines.text: Oh my goodness! You haven’t heard!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Heard what?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Lynch
lines.text: Joanna passed away last night.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God! How?!

lines.speaker: Mrs. Lynch
lines.text: Well, she was leaving work and she was hit by a cab.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God! Oh, I cannot believe it!

lines.speaker: Mrs. Lynch
lines.text: I know!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, God. Oh, God. Oh God.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Lynch
lines.text: I didn’t realize that she was so close.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes, so close. Mrs. Lynch, I know that this is an emotional and difficult time, for all of us. But by any chance did Joanna send any paperwork your way before… it happened.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Lynch
lines.text: No. Nothing. Imagine, if she had just stepped off that curb a few seconds later.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes-yes, just a few seconds and she’d still be with us—nothing about an assistant buyer?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Lynch
lines.text: No, I’m sorry. I have to go.
lines.stage_direction: starting to cry She leaves as Sophie arrives.

lines.speaker: Sophie
lines.text: Good morning!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, Sophie, I guess you didn’t hear about Joanna…

lines.speaker: Sophie
lines.text: I sure did!

scenes.scene_description: Rachel’s office, she is coming in for the day carrying a picture for her new office. Mrs. Lynch is coming out of Joanna’s office, carrying a box.

lines.speaker: Allesandro
lines.text: I’m so excited about having Monica come on board with us. Although I do feel bad about having fired chef Emillio, it’s like losing a member of the family. Of course, that literally is the case for several of you. Tony, Carlos, Marie, please, tell your father how much we’re gonna miss him. Now, I know that Monica has a lot of great ideas for this place, well, you all read the review. So without much further ado, I present to you our new head chef.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Umm, I just wanna say, uh that with a pinch of exictement, a dash of hard work, a dollup of cooperation, we can have the recipe... Are you gonna kill me?
lines.stage_direction: reads from a 3 X 5 card Looks up and sees eveyone glaring at her

scenes.scene_description: Monica’s new kitchen, Allesandro is introducing her to her new employees.

title: The One With The Girl From Poughkeepsie

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler, Joey, and Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I’m sorry I’m late, did I miss anything?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Joey stuffing 15 Oreos in his mouth.
lines.stage_direction: Joey, with an obvious mouth full, nods yes.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: 15? Your personal best!
lines.stage_direction: Joey nods again Ross takes an Oreo and Joey mumbles, no!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Where were you?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, on a date. Yeah, I met this girl on the train going to a museum upstate.

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Joey's, Joey, Chandler, and Phoebe are there as Ross enters.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, yeah! How did you meet her?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, which museum?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, answer his.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, it was just me and her at the back of the train, and I sat near the door, so she’d have to pass by me if she wanted to switch cars. She was totally at my mercy.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Were you so late because you were burring this woman?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, I’m getting back down ‘cause she lives in Poughkeepsie. She seems really great, but she’s like totally great, but she lives two and a half hours away.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: How can she be great if she’s from Poughkeepsie? That joke would’ve killed in Albany.
lines.stage_direction: laughs, at they all look at him

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Done! I did it! Heh, who’s stupid now?
lines.stage_direction: He smiles and has cookie remains all over his teeth.

scenes.scene_description: simultaneously

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, look at this! They’re lighting the big Christmas tree tonight.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Umm, that paper’s two weeks old.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right, who keeps leaving old newspapers in the trash?! I really wanted to take Kathy to this, I can’t believe I missed it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey, y’know, at least you have somebody to miss that stuff with! I hate being alone this time of year! Next thing you know it’ll be Valentine’s Day, then my birthday, then bang!—before you know it, they’re lighting that damn tree again. Ohh, I want somebody! Y’know, I want a man !! I mean, it doesn’t even have to be a big relationship, y’know, just like a fling would be great.
lines.stage_direction: hearing this, Gunther moves in Gunther leaves depressed

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Really?! I didn’t think girls ever just wanted a fling.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, believe me, it’s been a long time since I’ve been flung.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, I know what I’m giving you for Christmas.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y’know what? There’s some nice guys at my office, do you want me to set you up?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah! Wait a minute, it’s been a long time that I’ve been single. How come you never offered this before?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I have a girlfriend, I’m-I’m happy. So, I no longer feel the need to go out of my way to stop others from being happy.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay! No accountants. Oh, and no one from like legal. I don’t like guys with boring jobs.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh and Ross was like what? A lion tamer?

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Chandler, Joey, Rachel, and Phoebe are there.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What’s wrong Mon?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ohh, everybody at the restaurant still hates me.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I thought I was making headway, everyone was smiling at me all day, I get off work and I find out that they wrote this on my chef’s hat.
lines.stage_direction: puts on her chef hat The hat says ‘Quit, bitch’

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey, maybe they meant to write, ‘Quiet, bitch.’

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey, honey! What’s the matter? Fine, I was just trying to be nice! Whoa!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I mean I have not been picked on this much since kindergarten and they had to bring in someone from junior high to do the see-saw with me.
lines.stage_direction: Joey laughs and Monica glares at him.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ohhh!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I mean they’re trying to do everything they can to make me quit, and if there were any other job, I would. But this is something I’ve been waiting for my whole life.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, wait a minute, you’re the boss! Why don’t you just yell at them? Or, fire them?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I would love too, but I can’t! I mean I just can’t, you know that I’m not good at confrontation.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, you know what you can do? I remember reading about this director, I think it was Orson Wells, who at the beginning of the movie would hire somebody, just so he could fire them in front of everybody. Then they would all know, who’s boss.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, Mon! I’m not doing anything, why don’t you fire me?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That’s a good idea! Wait, do you know how to waiter?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Good enough to get fired.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, you’re hired!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! That must be why I got fired last week! Does this Orson Wells guy direct Burger King commercials?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes.
lines.stage_direction: he glares at him for a while

scenes.scene_description: Monica enters

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I say, Drew! Are you seeing anybody right now? Og-ee-op, I’m not asking for me, I’m… I mean… No, I’m-I’m not gay, I’m not asking you out. I’m not-I’m not-I’m not gay!

lines.speaker: Drew
lines.text: I didn’t think you were gay. I do now.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: See my friend-my friend, Rachel, she wants to be set up.

lines.speaker: Drew
lines.text: Ahh, I just got out of a big relationship, I’m not looking for any thing serious.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, y’know what, that might be okay even if it was just kind of a fling, that might be all right with Rachel.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Whoa-whoa-whoa-whoa! Is this, hot Rachel, that you took to the Christmas party, Rachel?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, by the way, that is her full name.

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Oh wow! I’m free for her!

lines.speaker: Drew
lines.text: Oh, wait a second! I didn’t say I wasn’t free!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Hey, Chandler, why don’t we talk this over at the Ranger game tomorrow?

lines.speaker: Drew
lines.text: Hold on, y’know I just got a box of Cubans, maybe I bring them by your office around uh, five?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh well, that’s uh, a little later than I uh, generally care to stay, but sure!

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.text: Maybe, before the game we could enjoy some eight year old some small batch Basel Hadens.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I don’t really know what that is, but let’s !!

scenes.scene_description: Chandler’s office, he is trying to find Rachel a date.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey! You guys, I’m writing a holiday song for everyone. Do you want to hear it?

lines.speaker: Monica, Rachel, and Joey
lines.text: Yes!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Happy Chanukah, Monica! May your Christmas be snowy, Joey! Happy New Year, Chandler and Ross. Spin the draddle, Rachel!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Pheebs, that’s great!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, yay!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But y’know umm, Rachel doesn’t rhyme with draddle.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know but it’s so hard! Nothing rhymes with your stupid name!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What are you talking about? Lots of things rhyme with Rachel. Bagel. Mail. Jail. Bail. Able. May-pole.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All good, thanks. Do you maybe have a nickname have like a nickname that’s easier to rhyme?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Didn’t your dad used to call you Pumpkin?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Pumpkin? Yeah. But did he ever call you like, Budolph?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hello, children!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Have I got the 50 guys for you!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yeah, I just showed this a picture of you and guys were throwing themselves at me! They’re buying me drinks! They’re giving me stuff! Knicks tonight?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Sure! Where are the seats?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wherever! I’ve got like 20!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So, will I like any of these guys?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y’know what, I’m gonna uh, play the field just a little more.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Chandler!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Guys are signing over their 401-K’s to me?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You work with robots!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes. Okay, there’s this one guy, Patrick, I think you’re gonna like him, he’s really nice, he’s funny, he’s a swimmer.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh, I like swimmer’s bodies!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, and his father invented that magnetic strip on the back of credit cards.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Op, I like credit cards!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: See, I’m not bad at this fixing up thing, huh?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, so what does he do?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, he works in the Fine Foods division.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Your company has a fine foods division?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It’s a big company, I don’t—if you—I…

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Now, wait a second! You make food and robots?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No! No, the robots just work for them.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, I’m gonna go to work. Does anybody have a problem with that?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, lady, I do! I got a problem with that!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You want a problem? I’ll give you a problem!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, what are you gonna do? You’re gonna fire me?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You bet your ass, I’m gonna fire you! Thank you.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's, Phoebe is working on a new song.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, wow! I should get going. I-I got a date tonight.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yeah! With who?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You know that girl I told you about who lives up in Poughkeepsie?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Not her. Yeah, this is someone else I meet, and I-I can’t decide between the two of them. Y’know the one from Poughkeepsie, even though she’s a two hour train ride away, is really pretty, really smart, and-and a lot of fun. But this other girl, well, she lives right uptown. Y’know she’s, well she’s-she’s just as pretty, I guess she’s smart, she’s not fun.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: If she’s no fun, why do you want to date her at all?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, I-I want to give her another chance, y’know? She lives so close. And, at the end of the date, the other time, she-she said something that was—if she was kidding was very funny. On the other hand, if she wasn’t kidding, she’s not fun, she’s stupid, and kind of a racist.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, man!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey! Ooh, how was your first day working at the restaurant?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Damn!
lines.stage_direction: checks his watch runs out to work

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Ross, Phoebe, and Chandler are there.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, what happened to your fancy chef’s jacket?
lines.stage_direction: sees there’s a burn spot on it

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: They baked it. I can’t take this anymore. I’m gonna call a meeting tonight, I’m gonna fire you tonight.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You got it! Oh-oh!
lines.stage_direction: He starts patting the burned spot, which just happens to be over her breast.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What are you doing?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It’s still a tiny bit on fire there.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Thanks. I think you got it!
lines.stage_direction: Joey’s still patting the burn spot

scenes.scene_description: Allesandro’s, Monica is cooking.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Chandler!! You have the best taste in men!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, like father, like son.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Patrick and I had such a great time last night! I mean I think this could maybe turn into something serious.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Really?! I-I thought you weren’t looking for something serious? I thought you were looking for some kind of a fling.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, y'know, possibly. You didn’t tell him that, though? Right?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ummmmmmmm, no.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You told this guy that I was looking for a fling?! You don’t tell the guy that!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why not?! I’d be thrilled if I heard that some hot girl was just looking to get—oh I see.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, between you telling him that I wanted to have a fling and me putting out on the first date—oh, he’s so gonna get the wrong idea.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Chandler is there.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, Joey, could you pass the cheese?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah. Listen uh, I’d prefer it if you didn’t call me Joey. Since I don’t know anyone here, I thought it’d be cool to try out a cool work nickname.

lines.speaker: A Waiter
lines.text: Hey, dragon! Here’s your tips from Monday and Tuesday.

scenes.scene_description: Allesandro’s, Joey is eating some cheese.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: There’s like-there’s like 300 bucks in this one!

lines.speaker: The Waiter
lines.text: Yeah, people get pretty generous around the holidays. And it never hurts to wear tight trousers.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay. Could the waiters gather around to hear tonight’s specials? Okay, first there is a Chilean Sea Bass prepared with a Mango relish on a bag—Why is nobody writing these down?

lines.speaker: The Waiter
lines.text: Because we can remember them.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Because your all gonna make up fake specials and make me cook them like you did the other night?

lines.speaker: The Waiter
lines.text: Well, sure, that too.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, forget the specials for a minute. Umm, all right here’s the thing, for the last two weeks I have umm, tried really hard to create a positive atmosphere…

lines.speaker: The Waiter
lines.text: Can’t hear you!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: A positive atmosphere! But I-I-I have had it up to here. From now on, it is gonna be my way, or the highway! All right? Does anybody have a problem with that?!! Hey new guy! I said, does anybody have a problem with that?!
lines.stage_direction: louder She holds her hand over her head as an afterthought. Joey looks at the money he’s holding, and doesn’t speak up.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No ma’am.

lines.speaker: The Waiter
lines.text: Hey! He has a name, it’s Dragon. Do you wanna know your name? Check your hat. We did the hat right?
lines.stage_direction: to another waiter The other waiter nods yes.

scenes.scene_description: hands him two envelopes

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What the hell happened?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I am so-so-so sorry. I was gonna do it! Really! But I was standing there with 327 dollars in one hand and 238 dollars in the other hand, and I was thinking, "Wow! It’s been a long time since I had… 327 + 238 dollars!"
lines.stage_direction: tries to do the math in his head, but can’t

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey, we had a deal. That-that’s why you’re here! I’ve got to fire you!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And I gotta pay rent! Look, how-how about this? You don’t fire me, instead I stay here, I gain their trust, and they’ll start listening to all the nice things I’ve been saying about you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What kinda things have you been saying?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well nothing yet, they really hate you and I want to fit in.

scenes.scene_description: Allesandro’s, continued from earlier. The other waiters are gone and Monica is confronting Joey about his not speaking up.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Happy, happy Chanukah, Chandler and Monica. Very merry…

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, y’know, y’know what Pheebs?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I’m not Jewish, so…

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So! Ross doesn’t really decorate his tree with floss, but you don’t hear him complaining do you? God!
lines.stage_direction: Phoebe hits her guitar which wakes up Ross with a start.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Bad dream?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I wasn’t sleeping.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yeah, then uh, what was Phoebe’s song about?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: The one with the cat. I gotta go, I’ve got another date.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So, did you pick one yet?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, it turns out that the one from uptown was making a joke. But it was a different joke than I thought—it wasn’t that funny. So I’m still torn.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well look, you don’t really like the one from uptown and you’re too exhausted from dating the one up in Poughkeepsie, so I say you just end them both. Okay? You take a train up to Poughkeepsie and break up with her, and on your way back you break up with uptown. And then by the time you get home tonight, you’re done!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Y’know, you’re right. Thank you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Umm, well I had a similar problem when I lived in Prague.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Prague?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: There’s sooo much you don’t know.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Phoebe is working on her holiday song, Chandler is sitting on the couch reading a magazine, and Ross is sleeping on the couch.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Chandler! Patrick just uh, ended things with me. Did you or did you not tell him that I was looking for a serious relationship?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I did! I absolutely did!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You idiot!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I’m sure you’re right, but why?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You don’t tell a guy that you’re looking for a serious relationship! You don’t tell the guy that! Now you scared him away!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, man. I’m sorry, I’m so-so sorry.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Y’know, you should never be allowed to talk to people!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I know! I know!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! See just I’m right back where I started! Aww, this sucks! Being alone, sucks!
lines.stage_direction: She sits down heavily in one of the new chairs

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, y’know, you’re-you’re gonna meet somebody! You’re a great catch! Y’know when I was telling all those guys about you, I didn’t have to lie once.
lines.stage_direction: He sits down on the arm of her chair

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah! You graduated Magma Ku Laude, right?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, it doesn’t matter. Hey, y’know what, I’ve got two tickets to tonight’s Rangers game, you wanna come with me?
lines.stage_direction: Kisses her on the top of her head.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Cute guys in little shorts? Sure.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, actually it’s a hockey team, so it’s angry Canadians with no teeth.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well that sounds fun too.

lines.speaker: Chandler
