lines.text: Oh my God, that’s so freaky! Turn him off!!

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Joey's erm, Monica and Rachel's, Phoebe and Monica are plugging in a bunch of electronics.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh, hey, could we put on the news? I think it might be raining.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, just hold on a second. I’m watching this rugby thing on ESPN . I don’t know what the big deal is. I’m man enough to play this sport.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Dude, you’re not even man enough to order the channel that carries the sport.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Hey there Ross!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Janice.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y’know uh, you didn’t really have to help me pack.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Ohh, well when you said all you were going to be doing between now and the time you leave is packing, you didn’t really leave me much choice. Did you?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I-I thought I did but, I-I guess I did not !

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey-hey, what’s going on?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, I’m packing. Y’know I’m-I’m packing ‘cause I’m moving to Yemen tomorrow.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Thanks for telling me!

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's erm, Chandler and Joey's, Ross, Joey, and Rachel are watching rugby on TV as Phoebe enters.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I’m only going to pretend I’m moving to Yemen, it’s the only way I can get rid off her.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ohhhh, good one! And Yemen that actually sounds like a real country.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Chandler! Come on, I’m gonna show how to roll up your underwear and stuff it in your shoes. It’s a real space saver.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I know, I do that ‘cause it makes me look taller.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Okay, Chandler, come on!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay. Joey, trade lives with me!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Nope. Man look at this! Ross, I can’t believe you said you’d play rugby. I mean look how brutal this is!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, I can handle it! All right?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Please, Ross, you-you got hurt playing badminton with my dad.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That’s ‘cause-‘cause you’re mom’s dog kept-kept looking at me.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, Ross, look-look-look-look, look right here. That’s called a scrum, okay? It’s kinda like a huddle.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And is a hum, kinda like a scruddle?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ross! They’re gonna kill you!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, why are you doing this anyway?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, you should’ve seen the guy that she used to go out with. I mean, he’s like Joe Rugby.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You’re kidding! And he plays rugby?! That’s so funny. Ohh! I see how you did that. All right.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Anyway, she thought the very idea of me playing rugby with him was like hilarious. So I’m gonna show her how tough I really am!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I’m sorry. I’m sorry. You’re right, you are a tough guy. You’re the toughest palaeontologist I know.
lines.stage_direction: starts laughing, Ross stares at her

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, come on look, Ross can take care of himself! It’s not like he’s…Chandler!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, don’t worry about me. Okay? I’ll just stay real energetic and stay away from the ball. I’ll uh, I’ll be that guy right out of the circle.

scenes.scene_description: Janice runs into the bedroom.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Oh!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, well maybe there was a dog lookin’ at him.

scenes.scene_description: He points to a player who starts running and then gets viscously tackled from behind.
Ross turns and wants to attack Rachel, but Joey stops him.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ross-Ross-Ross-Ross! Stay away from that guy , and that guy . And that one—Dude! They’re all huge!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: They don’t look any bigger than me!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, maybe that’s because you’re closer to you. So you look bigger to you from where you are.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: I’m just going to say hi to the lads. All right?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Okay.
lines.stage_direction: Goes to say hi to the lads.

scenes.scene_description: The park, everyone is warming up for the rugby game. Only Joey and Phoebe came to support Ross.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hi.

scenes.scene_description: A player comes over and picks up a ball in front of Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Whoa! I kinda liked it.
lines.stage_direction: The player leaves and to Joey

scenes.scene_description: The player stands up and smiles. Showing that he has no front teeth.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, I know what I have to do. I’ve got to go Red Ross. Y’know, Red Ross!
lines.stage_direction: Joey and Phoebe don’t know what he’s talking about.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I totally don’t know what you’re talking about.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come on! The time we were all waiting in line for Dances With Wolves and that one guy cut in line in front of us and I just lost it?! Screamed at him! Turned all red! Red Ross!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You’ll see.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Liam, do me a favour. Tell the lads to go easy on Ross, it’s his first time.

lines.speaker: Liam
lines.text: You don’t say!
lines.stage_direction: We see Ross who is hopping about with the ball and spikes it in his face.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Good luck, babe.

scenes.scene_description: The referee blows the whistle and the players gather to start the game.

lines.speaker: Liam
lines.text: Ross! Ross! Come on! Get in here! Ross! Come on!

scenes.scene_description: The scrum forms and the game is underway.

lines.speaker: Liam
lines.text: Ross, come on! Get in the bloody scrum! Ross, get in!

scenes.scene_description: Ross walks over to the scrum, walks around a bit looking for a way to get into the scrum.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: JOEY!!!!!!

scenes.scene_description: Ross, urged on by his team-mates, jumps on top of the scrum and falls headfirst into the middle, leaving his feet sticking straight up.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You…are…not going to believe it! Joshua came into work today, and guess what happened?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: He asked you out?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No. But I was showing him some cufflinks and I felt his pulse.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Saucy!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What are these?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Electrical plans for the building.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, okay, okay should I be scared?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know that switch does something, okay? So-so I went down to city hall and got these. All I had to do was pay $25 and wait in line for three hours.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wow! If only more people knew.

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Joey's erm, Monica and Rachel’s, Monica is going over some plans as Rachel enters.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y’know you, really didn’t have to take me to the airport.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Oh please. Every moment is precious. Y’know? Besides, somebody had to ride in that other taxi with the rest of your luggage, and your friends don’t really seem to care too much that you’re leaving.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, we’re really not that close. Okay, so I guess this is uh, good-bye then.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: On no! No! It’s not good-bye, I’m not leaving until you get on that plane.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay. Then I guess it’s just, wait here then. Hi. I need one fake ticket to Yemen.
lines.stage_direction: Hands her the sign that says, Wait Here." To the ticket agent

lines.speaker: Ticket Counter Attendant
lines.text: One ticket to Yemen?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh no-no-no, no. No, no, no, I just, I just need a pretend ticket.

lines.speaker: Ticket Counter Attendant
lines.text: I’m sorry sir, I don’t understand.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What would you give to a kid if he wanted a ticket to play with?

lines.speaker: Ticket Counter Attendant
lines.text: Are you travelling with a child?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No. All right, y’know what, she’s gonna think that I’m handing you a credit card, but what I’m really gonna do is hand you a library card.

lines.speaker: Ticket Counter Attendant
lines.text: Ah, sir a ticket to Yemen is $2,100 and we don’t take library cards.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: What’s the matter? Is something wrong? Do you have to stay?

scenes.scene_description: The airport, the Yemen Express counter, Chandler is still trying to get rid of Janice.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: I can’t believe they’re doing that to him! I told them to go easy on him!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No offence but, y’know sometimes it’s hard to understand you, y’know with the accent, so…

scenes.scene_description: The rugby game, Ross is getting killed.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: That’s just halftime, there’s more of this.

scenes.scene_description: The whistle blows.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Did you see me? I was pretty good, huh? That is one fun game!

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Right.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, could you do me a favour? Could you just grab me a bottle of water?

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thanks. I-I think I’m dying. I really do.
lines.stage_direction: When she’s gone he collapses into Joey.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, poor baby.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Tell my son that I love him. Excellent! Well, okay, I gotta have some more fun!
lines.stage_direction: to Phoebe Emily returns with the water.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Ross, they are killing you out there!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That’s not true!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: She’s right! You have to stop!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? No! No, I’m not stopping. I’m Red Ross!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Dude, if you go back out there, you’re gonna be Dead Ross!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don’t care! I am not quitting! I insist on finishing this game!

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: All right, all right, if you insist on doing this, at least let me help you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, God no! That is no place for a woman. Those guys will grab anything.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: No. That’s not what I’m saying. I just may know a few things that might help you inflict some pain.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I like that.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Yeah? Listen closely, Devon has got a weak ankle.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Huh?

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: One swift kick and he’ll back off.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right, bad ankle, got it!

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: And that big bloke with the beard, he has got a trick hip. Yeah. And uh, and David over there, I heard he doesn’t wear a cup.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah? I can use that, trick hip, no cup, okay! Okay!

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: And uh, Liam, Liam’s got bad knees. You hit him right and he’ll go down like a lamp.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: But-but, Liam’s on my team.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: I don’t care! You just get him!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I’m gonna go get him! Okay, I am going back in!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: The Red Ross! Okay.

scenes.scene_description: Ross limps over all covered in mud.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What-what are-what are these?
lines.stage_direction: noticing a bunch of pictures around the door that weren’t there originally.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, just some pictures I made and hung up. I thought they’d brighten up the place. They do don’t you think?

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Joey's erm, Monica and Rachel’s, Monica is reading a book as Rachel returns.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No-no-no, no!

scenes.scene_description: Rachel rips one of the wall and finds a huge hole underneath.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know that there’s no hole there, I just really liked that picture.

scenes.scene_description: Rachel rips off another one, revealing another hole. Rachel then moves onto a third one, but this one doesn’t have a hole underneath it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God! Look at this!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, but there is a wire back there! I mean that switch is connected to something!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don’t care! The wires have come loose in your head!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I just thought that if I could follow the wire I could find out what it did.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And did you?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No. It disappears back there behind that baseboard. For a minute there, I thought it went downstairs.

scenes.scene_description: Rachel looks at it and then throws it away. She then removes a fourth one, revealing a third hole.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: But it didn’t. Say hello to Mrs. Chatracus.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Chatracus
lines.text: Hello darling.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hello, Mrs. Chatracus.

scenes.scene_description: Rachel removes a paper on the floor which is covering a hole and gasps.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Now, are you sure you don’t want to go see a doctor?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh no! That-that’ll just bring me down! This was great! I mean I-I-I was great! This is a great day! Y’know what? I’m buying everyone coffee. All right? If someone would just grab my wallet, it’s in my pocket.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, sure.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, not you.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uhh, look, your eye’s still popping out a little, I’m gonna go get some ice.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh, ice! I am so in the mood for ice!
lines.stage_direction: They go and get the coffee and the ice leaving Ross and Emily alone.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: You were amazing out there.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, I kinda was, wasn’t I?

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I made a man twice my size cry. I mean, I haven’t done that since I was four and I washed my dad’s Porsche with rocks.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: You really enjoyed yourself didn’t you?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Please! Are you kidding? I-I hurt three huge men, I gave a guy a bloody nose—I mean I-I’m not proud of it but, I really am. And it’s all because of you, wonderful, amazing you.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: I think you’ve got concussion.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, no, I’m serious. Thank you.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: You’re welcome. I’m sorry. Did I hurt you?
lines.stage_direction: She hugs him tightly and he winces.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It’s worth the pain. Y’know what, you know what? It’s not.
lines.stage_direction: She goes to hug him again.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, They are returning from the rugby game, Joey and Emily are carrying Ross.

lines.speaker: Ticket Counter Attendant
lines.text: This is the final boarding call for Flight 664 to Yemen.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I-I guess I gotta go.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Oh, my Bing-a-ling. I’ll wait for you. Do you even know how long you’re going to be gone?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, just until we find an energy source to replace fuel.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Oh. Well, I’ll right you everyday. 15 Yemen Road, Yemen.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, good-bye. Good-bye.

scenes.scene_description: The airport, the flight to Yemen is being called.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Chandler?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Chandler!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Janice! There you are! There you are! I had to have one last kiss, and also-also you said that you were going to leave right after I got on the plane!

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: No! No! I wanna see you take-off.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I then guess I’m going to Yemen! I’m going to Yemen! When we get to Yemen, can I stay with you?
lines.stage_direction: To this old woman also going to Yemen.

scenes.scene_description: He gives the agent his ticket and walks onto the jetway. Janice walks over and looks out the window. Chandler walks back into the terminal and tries to walk right past Janice, but she sees him.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right. The super couldn’t figure out what it did. A $200 an hour electrician couldn’t figure out what it did. I’ve had seven pretty serious shocks. I officially give up.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thank God.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I guess Joey was right, it does nothing.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: See? I’m doing it. I am totally doing it. I lost it.

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Joey's erm, Monica and Rachel’s, Monica has finally given up on her search to find what the light switch does and is now flicking it on and off aimlessly.

title: The One With The Fake Party

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What is it hon?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I-I can’t find anything that I want to eat! Everything I eat makes me nauseous! I’m telling you, being pregnant is no piece of cake—ooh! Cake! No.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Aww, honey I’m sorry.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: God! Ooh! What is that smell? It’s coming from the bathroom. Ooh!

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's erm, Chandler and Joey's, Chandler, Monica, and Phoebe are there. Phoebe is stressing out about something.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wow! Pregnancy does give you some weird cravings.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It’s me. It’s Phoebe. Listen there’s something in here I want to eat, what-what smells so good?

scenes.scene_description: She goes to the bathroom.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Is it the shampoo? It’s guava.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh! Wait-wait! Is it my bologna sandwich?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes. Yes. Yes. I can’t believe it! The baby wants bologna! Maybe he wants me to eat meat? I can’t eat meat!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, wait-wait! Maybe it’s a pickle?!

scenes.scene_description: Joey sticks his head out of the shower curtain.
Phoebe grimaces at the smell.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What are you writing?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, Joshua’s coming in tomorrow and since I don’t have the guts to ask him out, I’m going to sell him a coat and put this note in the pocket.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yeah? Joshua, give me a call sometime, guys like you never go out of style—what did you throw away?
lines.stage_direction: He grabs the pad and starts reading it. Pause

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Chandler, Monica, Joey and Rachel are there. Rachel is writing something on a pad, and then crumples it up and throws it on the floor.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hi guys!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, what have you guys been up too?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, we went to see a collection of Victorian doorknobs and the Cupert-Hewitt museum.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Without me?!

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: My uncle dragged us there. But, it actually it turned out to be really interesting.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: They were so ornate and beautiful, I mean look at that!
lines.stage_direction: Shows them a doorknob she has.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don’t know how museums work in England but, here, you’re not supposed to take stuff.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: I uh, I got it from the gift shop. They have really lax security there. It’s a joke.
lines.stage_direction: Chandler is shocked. They all laugh.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Bye.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Right, I’ve got to be off, I’ll see ya. Buh-bye then.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wow! You guys seem to be having a good time.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh yeah, she’s-she’s amazing. And-and she’s so much fun. And! Y’know what? When I’m with her, I’m fun! I even signed up for helicopter classes. She’s leaving in two days, I don’t have to do it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh no, two days, you must be bummed.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, she’s got to go back to London. But you know what? I’ve been prepared for this from the start. We both knew we had two weeks together, and that’s it. Y’know.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey that’s what all my relationships are like.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, but in Ross’s case, they both know in two weeks that’s it.

scenes.scene_description: Ross and Emily enter.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Pheebs!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hello! Hello!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes! I know! I know! Yeah! So the baby is totally craving meat. This afternoon I tried tricking it, I made it a soy-burger to make it think it was getting meat, y’know? And I got nauseous.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Maybe that’s because soy-burgers suck!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Being pregnant is tough on your tummy.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, but at least you got that cool, pregnant lady glow.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That’s sweat. You throw up all morning, you’ll have that glow too.

scenes.scene_description: Phoebe enters.

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Okay!
lines.stage_direction: coming in from a changing room

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, here’s that trench-coat that you wanted.

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Oh great! Wow! Yeah, it’s comfortable.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Man, I could really flash somebody in this thing.
lines.stage_direction: He goes to put his hands in his pockets.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh no-no, no-no, they don’t want you to put your hands in the pockets until you are out of the store.

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Why not?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, that’s because of a lot of…
lines.stage_direction: She imitates someone picking their nose and placing the treasure found in the pockets.

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Y’know, they ruin it for everybody.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know!

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Y’know, I wore that cashmere sweater on a date last night.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh?

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Yeah, it was my first date since the uh, since the divorce.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, congratulations, so do you love her?

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: No, no, no, she’s nice but, y’know, it just it made me realize that I’m just not, I’m just not ready to be dating, y’know?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Huh. Well, uh, that’s uh, that’s interesting.
lines.stage_direction: She goes over and retrieves her note.

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Hey-whoa-hey-hey, what was that?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, it’s just an anti-theft device.

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Then uh, what’s-what’s this?
lines.stage_direction: Shows her the real anti-theft device.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You need that, you need that too ‘cause obviously, a thief could just tear this up.

scenes.scene_description: Bloomingdale’s , Rachel is preparing to slip Joshua the note.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oops, sorry. Listen, we-we have to have a party tonight! Actually, we have to have one in five minutes, so everybody cancel your plans.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What are you talking about?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, what’ going on?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: We have to have a surprise Bon Voyage party for Emily. But it’s actually for Joshua. Look, he said he’s not ready to date, so I had to invite him to a party if I wanted to see him outside of work, and now I have the perfect opportunity to seduce him!

scenes.scene_description: Chandler’s bedroom, he is sleeping with Marjorie. All of the sudden, Marjorie starts talking in her sleep, awakening Chandler. After a little bit, she quiets back down, and Chandler tries to get back to sleep. There’s a short pause until she starts screaming, causing Chandler to scream with her. She quickly calms down. This all wakes up Joey, who comes over wearing the mouth guard, opens the top half of Chandler’s door, and starts to complain about the noise.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You don’t have a car. And your license expired.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know. Yeah, see, there’s so much to do and I have so little time to do it in.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's, the gang is watching the Prom Video and Rachel is about to make her entrance on the video. The italics are portions of the prom video.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right, check it out. Check this out. It says here that there’s a place you can go to rent videos of all the museums! "It’s almost as good as being there."

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It’s better! You can’t go to a museum in your underwear!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, You could, but... probably just the one time.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I bet we could get videos of all the sites, get a VCR in our hotel room... we'd never even have to go outside!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: If we do that, we gotta get Die Hard.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh-ho! I bet the British version is gooooood!

title: The One With The Worst Best Man Ever

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That’s like the tenth time I’ve peed since I’ve been here!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That’s also like the tenth time you told us.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, oh I’m sorry, it must be really hard to hear! I tell ya, it’s a lot easier having three babies play Bringing in the Noise, Bringing in da Funk on your bladder! I’m so sick of being pregnant! The only happiness I get is from a cup of coffee, which of course is decaf, ‘cause—Oh! I’m pregnant!

lines.text: Pheebs, did…you want a cookie?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Thank you so much.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So uh, Pheebs, honey, how are those mood swings coming?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I haven’t really had any yet.

Monica, Joey, and Chandler all shake their heads.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey guys!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right, here’s the ring.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes! Yes! A thousand times, yes!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So uh, any ideas for the bachelor party yet?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Whoa-whoa-whoa! Before you start handing out wedding rings and planning bachelor parties, don’t you have to decide who your best man is gonna be?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, it’s awkward. It’s awkward. It’s awkward.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I sort’ve already asked Chandler.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?! He got to do it at your first wedding!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Joey, I figured you’d understand. I mean, I-I’ve known him a lot longer.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come on Ross! Look, I-I don’t have any brothers; I’ll never get to be a best man!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You can be the best man when I get married.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I’ll never get to be a best man!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wait-wait, so, you get to be my best man twice and I never get to be yours at all?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh no-no-no, you—yeah, of course you get to be my best man.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What about me?! You-you just said I could!
lines.stage_direction: impatiently tapping Chandler on the shoulder

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I’m not even getting married! Okay, this is a question for science fiction writers!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I can’t believe you’re not picking me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, how can it not be me?!HeyHey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I’m not even… I’m not even…

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Fine, y’know what, that’s it. From now on, Joey, I want you to be my best man.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yes! Shame about you man.

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Joey's, Joey and Chandler are there as Ross enters.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Stop it!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: One of the babies is kicking.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I thought that was a good thing.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It’s not kicking me, it’s kicking one of the other babies. Oh ! Don’t make me come in there!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Do you guys have like a big bowl I can borrow?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, there’s one right under the cabinet.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Why do you need it?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, we’re having a big party tomorrow night. Later!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Whoa! Hey-hey, you planning on inviting us?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Nooo, later.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!! Get your ass back here, Tribbiani!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hormones!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What Phoebe meant to say was umm, how come you’re having a party and we’re not invited?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, it’s Ross’s bachelor party.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Sooo?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Are you bachelors?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Nooo!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Are you strippers?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Nooo!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Then you’re not invited.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right fine! You’re not invited to the party we’re gonna have either.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh-whoa, what party?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well umm…

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: The baby shower for Phoebe!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Baby shower. Wow! That sounds sooo like something I don’t want to do! Later!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I can’t believe I’m gonna have a party! This is so great! A party! Yay!! I don’t know why.
lines.stage_direction: Really excited Really, really excited Suddenly, she starts crying and Rachel moves to comfort her.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's, Monica, Rachel, and Phoebe are eating breakfast.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: This is what I’ve got going for the party so far, liquor wise. Get a lot of liquor.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Great. Great.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, now uh, in terms of the invite list, I’ve got you, me, and Chandler and I’m gonna invite Gunther ‘cause, well, we’ve been talking about this pretty loud.

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: I’ll be there.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right—oh! Listen, I know this is your party, but I’d really like to the number of museum geeks that are gonna be there.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah. Tell ya what, let’s not invite any of the anthropologists, only the dinosaur dudes!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay! We’ll need a six-pack of Zima .

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey guys, what are you doing?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, just planning my bachelor party with my best man.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, well, good luck trying to top the last one.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, see, I don’t think it’s gonna that difficult considering this one won’t be taking place in the basement of a Pizza Hut .

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, I’m Ross. I’m Ross. I’m too good for the Hut; I’m too good for the Hut.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, I gotta go pick up Ben. Everything so far sounds great Joey, just remember to keep it on the mellow side, okay? Just a couple of guys hanging out playing poker, no-no strippers or anything okay?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You got it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, see ya later.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: See ya. Have fun planning your mellow bachelor party.
lines.stage_direction: Ross exits, and Chandler moves over next to Joey, laughing.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, there’s gonna be strippers there. He didn’t say anything about no strippers.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: He just said, "No strippers."

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, I chose not to hear that.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Look what I got! Look what I got! Look what I got! Can you believe they make these for little people?
lines.stage_direction: She shows Rachel what she bought. She bought a little leather jacket and a little cowboy outfit for the babies.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Little village people.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, look at this one. This is my favourite.
lines.stage_direction: It’s a little pink and white dress for the girl baby.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, that is so sweet!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know! Phoebe is gonna love dressing them in these!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Huh. Except, Phoebe’s not gonna be the one that gets to dress them.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Because she’s not gonna get to keep the babies.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God! We are throwing the most depressing baby shower ever!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wait a minute! Unless, we give her all gifts she can use after she’s done being pregnant. Like-like umm, regular coffee, Tequila.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, and somebody can get those leather pants she’s always wanted!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, she’s gonna love that!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What the hell is this?! What, did you actually thought it would make me feel better to give me stuff that I can’t use for another two months?! This sucks! All right, what’s my next present?!

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's, Phoebe’s baby shower, she is holding those leather pants, and isn’t happy about it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey listen man, about the stripper…

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Good call!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, a little announcement, a little announcement. I’ve decided that my best man is, my best friend Gunther!
lines.stage_direction: banging a spoon against his beer bottle

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: What’s my last name?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Central Perk?

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: Thanks for not marrying Rachel.
lines.stage_direction: to Ross He starts to leave.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh-whoa-wait, Gunther don’t-don’t forget your shirt.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey-hey, what are those?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, little party favours, check it out!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow! Yeah!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh-oh!
lines.stage_direction: Shows him what’s on the back, "Best Man Joey Tribbiani, with a huge picture of him.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, okay, a little announcement, I just want everyone to know that the position of my best man is still open! And uh, so is the position of the bride.
lines.stage_direction: banging on the bottle again to the stripper

lines.speaker: The Stripper
lines.text: Great!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Smooth man. Yeah, you got some chilie on your neck. Well, I just want to say, thanks everyone, this-this was great. And hey! See you guys Monday morning. Thanks Joey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, hey, don’t forget your shirt.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, thanks!
lines.stage_direction: Takes it and throws it back into the box and leaves.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, hey, museum geeks, party’s over. Okay. Wave bye-bye to the nice lady. There you go. Back to your parent’s basement. All right. Come on boys, come on out! Here you go. All right.
lines.stage_direction: The museum geeks exit and Joey unlocks his door and lets the chick and the duck out.

lines.text: Ohhh, look at the little birdies! Are those yours?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: The Stripper
lines.text: Wow, I didn’t know they let you keep chickens and ducks as pets.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh yeah-yeah. And I got the duck totally trained. Watch this. Stare at the wall. Hardly move. Be white.
lines.stage_direction: The duck complies. The duck complies. The duck complies.

lines.speaker: The Stripper
lines.text: You are really good at that. So uh, I had fun tonight, you throw one hell of a party.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh thanks. Thanks. It was great meetin’ ya. And listen if any of my friends gets married, or have a birthday, or a Tuesday…

lines.speaker: The Stripper
lines.text: Yeah, that would be great. So I guess umm, good night.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh unless you uh, you wanna hang around.

lines.speaker: The Stripper
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah. I’ll let you play with my duck.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, stripper!
lines.stage_direction: realises he doesn’t know her name. He notices that the ring box is open, so he picks it up, sees it’s empty and starts to panic.

scenes.scene_description: Joey’s bedroom, it’s the middle of the night, he’s waking up and discovers he’s alone in bed.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: The stripper stole the ring!! The stripper stole the ring!! Chandler! Chandler, get up! Get up! The stripper stole the ring!
lines.stage_direction: running and banging on Chandler’s door

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: The ring is gone!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ugh. Just a sec, give me a minute to wake up for this—Ah-ha-ha!! You lost the ring! You’re the worst best man ever!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Dude, this isn’t funny! What am I gonna do?! I go to bed last night, everything’s cool! I wake up this morning, the stripper’s gone and the ring is gone!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You slept with the stripper?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Of course!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hi, guys.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi! Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I-I wanted to apologise if I—y’know seemed a tad edgy yesterday at my shower. Y’know it’s just the hormones, y’know.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No we…

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hormones.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: …hormones, yeah.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Anyway, I just wanted to say thank you, it was just, it was so sweet.

scenes.scene_description: Central Perk, Phoebe is entering, Monica and Rachel are talking on the couch.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wow, you seem to be doing so much better. That’s great. So how-how are things going?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Good. Y’know—no-no, okay, it’s-it feels like everything’s been about me lately, so what’s happening with you?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, well, actually we were just talking about me not going to Ross’s wedding.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It just might be too hard, given the history and all that…

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wow! This reminds me of the time when I was umm, living on the street and this guy offered to buy me food if I slept with him.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, h-how is this like that?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, let’s see, it’s not. Really, like that. Because, you see that was an actual problem, and uh, yours is just like y’know a bunch of y’know high school crap that nobody really gives y’know…

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I’m-I’m sorry, I just thought that…

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Alrighty, here come the water works.

scenes.scene_description: She goes to hug them and they both flinch, thinking that Phoebe is about to attack them.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ugh! I don’t know what I’m going to do! I called the company that sent and th-they don’t care! Then I called 9-1-1 and they laughed at me, if this isn’t an emergency, then what is?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey guys!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey…

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I just wanted to thank you again for last night, what a great party! And the guys from work had a blast. Y’know, one of them had never been to a bachelor party before. Yeah! And-and another one had never been to a party before, so…

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So uh, hey, that uh, that wedding ring, huh? Man, that’s nice!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, right!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I was uh, I was thinking I might want to pick one of those babies up for myself, I might want to get one of those…

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That ring? When my grandmother first came to this country, that ring and the clothes on her back were all she had with her.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So you might say, the ring is irreplaceable?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh absolutely! It has been in my family for generations, and every bride who has worn it has had a long and happy life.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So you might say, it’s a magic ring.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, the stripper stole it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: My-my ring? My-my wedding ring? The-the stripper stole my wedding ring?! H-how?! How could this all happen?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I think it all started when you said, "Hey Joey, why don’t you be my best man."

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right-all right, fine! I-I’m gonna call the cops!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Dude, I screwed up, you don’t have to turn me in!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Not on you! On the stripper!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, yeah, well I already did that! They said they’re gonna look into it right after they solved all the murders.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, well, we’ll call the company that sent her!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I did that too! They wouldn’t give me her real name or her number. They said, "If I bothered them again they’d call the police." I said, "If you talk to the police, you tell them I’m missing a ring!"

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So what, Joey? Wh-wh-what? What are you telling me? That there’s nothing we can do? Well, how could this happen?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look Ross, I am so-so sorry. I-I-I…

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, what if we just ah, called her, used a fake name, and had her come to my office?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, that sounds like fun, but we’ve got a ring to find!!

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Joey's, Joey is trying to figure out what to do.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Here’s your tea Phoebe.
lines.stage_direction: They give it to her and quickly take a step back.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It’s so good. Oh, thanks.
lines.stage_direction: sips it Monica and Rachel breath a sigh of relief.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Good.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I’m so glad you liked it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh!
lines.stage_direction: sets the cup down Grabs her stomach in pain.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?! She made the tea!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh! No, I-I think I just had a contraction.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You what?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I thought I had one a couple of minutes ago, and now I know that was definitely one.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wait, you can’t have the baby here! I mean I haven’t sterilised it since the guys moved out!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. It’s okay. We’re gonna be okay. Y’know what? It’s okay. I’m gonna, I’m gonna, I’m gonna boil some water and just rip up some sheets!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No. It’s all right; it’s probably false labour. They said that, that can happen near the end, just somebody get the book.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Rachel, get the book! The book!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay! Okay! Here!
lines.stage_direction: Runs and grabs a book and hands it to Monica.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: The Bible?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don’t know!

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's, Monica and Rachel are cautiously serving Phoebe some tea.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, okay, this is great, uh, Chandler, you get behind the desk. And-and when she comes in hopefully, she won’t recognise you because, well, why would she? Uh, okay, and then you buzz Ross and I. You be Mr. Gonzalez, and I’ll be uh, Mr. Wong.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Diverse.

scenes.scene_description: Chandler’s office, the guys are there waiting to ambush the stripper.

lines.speaker: The Stripper
lines.text: Did anybody call for security?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You be cool.
lines.stage_direction: to Ross He opens the door and lets her in as they all turn there backs on her.

lines.speaker: The Stripper
lines.text: Okay, which one of you guys is Gunther Central-Perk? Hey, Joey?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Where’s my ring? My dead grandmother’s wedding ring? Where is it? Where is it?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Way to be cool, man.

lines.speaker: The Stripper
lines.text: What’s he talking about?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: There was a ring, in a box, on my nightstand, after you left, it was gone!

lines.speaker: The Stripper
lines.text: Wait, you guys think I stole some ring?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: We know you took so just-just save yourself the time and confess!

lines.speaker: The Stripper
lines.text: Okay, who are you? The Hardy boys? Look, I don’t need to steal some stupid ring, all right? I make $1,600 a week doing what I do; any of you guys make that?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Marry me.
lines.stage_direction: Both Ross and Chandler hit him.

scenes.scene_description: There’s a knock on the door.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don’t get it! It was in my room all night! And if she didn’t take it, and I didn’t take it; and you didn’t take it, then who did? Shh! We’re trying to think!
lines.stage_direction: Chandler The duck quacks. Ross and Chandler realise it at the same moment and stare at Joey, who doesn’t get it. After a short pause, with the duck still quacking, Joey figures it out and starts pointing at the duck.

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Joey's, the guys are now trying to figure out what next to do, since their plan with the stripper backfired on them.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I still don’t get how you know when it’s false labour.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, do you see any babies?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How do you feel?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, I guess. I mean… I don’t know, it’s just, I guess I know it’s going to be over soon.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, isn’t that a good thing? You said you were sick of this.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know. It’s just y’know usually when you’re, when you’re done with the pregnant thing, y’know, then you get to do the mom thing. I’m gonna be y’know, sitting around in my leather pants, drinking Tequila.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Some moms do that.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay that’s even sadder. Look, I know, I know what I got myself into, it’s just that now that they’re in me it’s like, it’s like I know them y’know, I mean-I mean, it’s just not gonna be easy when these little babies have to go away.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Aww, sweetie, but it’s not like you’re not gonna have anything. You’re gonna have nieces and nephews, and some ways that’s even better.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, okay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, really. Really, Pheebs, you’re not gonna be the one worrying about saving for college, or yelling at them when they’re bad, y’know, or deciding to put them on Ritalin when they just won’t calm down. Y’know?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I mean, you’re the one they’re gonna come to when they wanna run away from home, and the one they talk to about sex.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And you just get to be cool Aunt Phoebe!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Cool Aunt Phoebe. I am pretty cool!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And y’know what else, oh my God, are they gonna love you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: They are gonna love me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Thanks you guys! Again.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, sweetie!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You’re the best. Thanks. Oh!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Just kidding. Ahh!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Got cha again, you guys are so easy.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's, Phoebe is recovering from her false labour.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: If anything should happen to him…

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Joey! The vet said it’s a simple procedure.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So! Things can go wrong! You don’t know! What if he doesn’t make it?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: He will, Joe.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, but what if he doesn’t? He’s such a good duck.

scenes.scene_description: The Animal Hospital, the guys have taken the duck in to remove the ring. Joey is pacing around like an expectant father.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I’m so worried about him, y’know?

lines.speaker: The Doctor
lines.text: Somebody lose a ring?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh my God! Thank you! Thank you so much!
lines.stage_direction: He grabs the ring, kisses it, and then does a double-take realising where it’s been.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: H-h-h-how’s the duck?

lines.speaker: The Doctor
lines.text: He’s doing just fine, he’s resting now, but you can see him in a little bit.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ohh, great! Oh hey, listen Ross, thanks for being so cool about this.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, that’s all right.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, it’s not. I mean you-you made me your best man and I totally let you down!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, come on, it’s not your fault.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, it is! You wouldn’t have lost the ring, right? Y’know what, Ross you were right from the start, he should be your best man.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, you should.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Now, don’t argue with me…

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey! Hey! Hey! I get to choose my best man, and I want both you guys.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, both you guys should be up there with me. I mean, you two are-are my… I mean, I’m lucky to have just one good…

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Thanks man.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I gotta go check something over here.
lines.stage_direction: starting to cry He walks away so that they can’t see him cry.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What a baby.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Total wuss!

scenes.scene_description: With that we go into a little flashback about the guys’ memories of the duck. The first one is Joey playing with him in the bathtub and drying him off. Then it’s Chandler sitting on his couch after they moved into the girls apartment, and Chandler reading to him in bed, and him watching Baywatch when all they had was the canoe and the duck was in a bucket of water. Then we see Ross eating some cereal and the duck watching him. He takes a lamp and moves the duck off of the table. Then it’s Chandler shooing them out of the bathroom in the girls’ apartment, Joey revealing their disco cubby hole in the entertainment-center, then Chandler playing Hide-and-Go-Seek with them, and it’s concluded with various scenes with the duck flapping it’s wings. And the guys staring into the distance in remembrance of the duck.
They both turn and wipe their eyes.

title: The One With Ross's Wedding Parts I and II

lines.speaker: Part I Written by
lines.text: Michael Borkow

lines.speaker: Part II Teleplay by
lines.text: Shana Goldberg-Meehan & Scott Silveri

lines.text: Eric Aasen

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Guys, hurry up! The flight leaves in four hours! It could take time to get a taxi! There could be traffic! The plane could leave early! When we get to London, there could be a line at customs! Come on!!

lines.text: Six-hour trip to London. That’s a lot of Monica.

lines.text: Passport, check! Camera, check! Traveller’s cheques, check!

lines.text: Myself. Y’know for remembering to pack a thing. Yeah, you do a good thing, you get a check! My mom does it, I never realized it was weird.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, my mom used to put her head in the oven. Well, actually, she only did it the one time. But it was pretty weird.

lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey! Are you ready yet?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yep! You got the tickets?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh! Got ‘em right here, check!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It’s all London, baby! Here we go.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You got your passport?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, in my third drawer on my dresser. You don’t want to lose that.

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Joey's, Joey and Chandler are getting ready for the flight to London and Monica comes running in.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ohh!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: There it is.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, if you need the vacuum, it’s in my closet on the left-hand side. Ah, the garbage bags are next to the refrigerator…

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, okay, but Rachel’s gonna be here too, can’t I just ask her this stuff?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, okay, give that a try!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right! Let’s do it!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Woo-hoo!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, cheerie-o!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: London baby!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, ‘cause that’s not gonna get annoying.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: London baby!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, y’know what? I was wrong.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, we’re all here! I guess we should get going!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ohhh, I wanna come over there and give you a hug and wish you luck on your wedding, but I don’t—can’t get up.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, I’ll-I’ll come hug you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Great! Yeah, could you bring me the newspaper?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.
lines.stage_direction: He does so and Phoebe hugs him.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, have a great wedding!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, hey, Chandler I wanna hug you too!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, and great! You might as well bring me my book, it’s on the counter in your apartment.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh.
lines.stage_direction: Goes and gets Phoebe’s book as Rachel comes in from her room.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So, we’re off.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Have fun!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thanks! Ugh, I can’t believe you’re not gonna be there!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I know.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So-so come! Why don’t you come?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: To London! Come to London. Please? It’ll mean so much to me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, well, I gotta work, I’m sorry.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Why-why can’t you take a couple of days off?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Because, I can’t! Ross, I told you, no. I can’t.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: This is my wedding.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, y’know what? We really are late! Let’s go! Let’s go! Let’s go!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Fine. You’ll-you’ll watch it on video when we get back.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Here you go Phoebe! Here you go Phobo! Phewbedo! Phaybobo.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Ross

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Bye, Pheebs!

lines.text: London baby!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, do you need a hug? You don’t have to bring me anything!

scenes.scene_description: They all start out, Rachel gives each one a kiss, and says "bye." In the hall, Joey says…

And with that, television history is made as, for the first time ever, an entire show moves it’s entire production to an entirely different country to make a single episode. We get shots of Buckingham Palace, London Bridge, Big Ben, and the London Marriott as Joey and Chandler exit.

lines.text: Come on! Do something!

lines.text: Oh, what the hell is that?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It’s London, baby! All right, the hotel’s here. Wait. No, we wanna go… No. I know. I’m gonna have to go into the map.

lines.text: I got it! Here we go.

lines.text: …and that was all before 10 o’clock. The caterer rang and said it was going to be Chicken Kiev instead of Chicken Tarragon. And then the florist phoned to say there aren’t any tulips. Oh, and the chilliest has carpel-tunel syndrome. We’re not gonna be…

lines.text: Sorry.

lines.text: Yeah. Yeah. Yes, this place is beautiful. Emily’s parents got married here.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Oh. Oh.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Oh. Oh my God. How can this be happening? What are we going to do?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It’s all right! Everything’s gonna be all right.

lines.text: How’s it gonna be all right?!

lines.text: Okay, I talked to the guy with the shovel and I found out what happened.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? What?

lines.text: All right! Westminster Abbey! Hands down, best Abbey I’ve ever seen. Hey! Okay. What do you think of the Abbey, Chandler?

lines.speaker: Joey

lines.text: Oh. Oh.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, honey! Don’t get up! What do you need?

lines.text: Okay, I have a wedgie.

lines.speaker: Rachel

lines.speaker: Phoebe

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Eh, don’t be so hard on yourself. If someone I was still in love with was getting married…

I mean, hey, y’know, I like Ross as much as the next guy, y’know? Clearly I have feelings for him, but feelings don’t mean love! I mean, I still have loving feelings for Ross. Yeah! But, I have, I have continuing feelings of love, but that doesn’t mean that-that I’m still in love with him. Y’know? I-I have sexual feelings for him, but I do love him—Ohh! Oh my God! Oh my—why didn’t you tell me?!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I can not believe you didn’t tell me!

lines.speaker: Phoebe

lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, wait. All right, that’s it, okay, I’m out of here. I am not going to be embarrassed anymore!

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Hello?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey! I just spoke to your dad, and you know what? He seems to think we’ll be able to find a new place for the wedding.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: We don’t have to.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Whoa-whoa-what? You found a place?

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: No. But, Monica and I were talking, and-and I was so upset about the hall being knocked down, and she suggested that we put the wedding off for a bit.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: She said what?

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: She said, "If I’m not gonna be happy getting married somewhere that we find in a day, well then we should just postpone it."

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Postpone it? Emily, do you think Monica realises how much our parents spent on this wedding? Do you my sister’s teeny-tiny little brain comprehends that people took time out of their lives to fly thousands of miles to be here, huh? This isn’t right.
lines.stage_direction: He puts his pants on backwards.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: I realize that people are going to be disappointed. But, I’m sure they’ll come back when we can do it right.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I can’t ask people to do that? Would you ask people to do that?

scenes.scene_description: The hotel, Ross’s room, Emily is entering.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Don’t you point your pants at me! We have no choice! Anywhere that’s half-decent would’ve be booked months ago, Ross don’t you understand? This is our wedding I’m talking about.
lines.stage_direction: She throws them on the floor.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: The only thing I understand is; postponing it is not an option. This is when we’re getting married.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: So what are you saying? It’s now or never?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No. I’m saying it’s now.
lines.stage_direction: He starts putting on his pants, backwards again.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Or?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: There’s no ‘or’ in mind. What is wrong with these pants?!!

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: It’s not the pants. It’s you that is backwards. And if, and if you don’t understand how important this is to me, well then, perhaps we shouldn’t get married at all!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, wait! Emily! No, wait, stop! Emily, please—
lines.stage_direction: chasing her, trying to zip up his pants. His got them on right now. He catches something important in the zipper and howls like a little boy and falls to the floor.

scenes.scene_description: Holds out his pants

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So, did shopping make you feel any better about Ross?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Manhattan does not have enough stores.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I think I can help you get over him.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You can?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah. I just need you to bring me some photos of Ross.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Um-hmm.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: And a small piece of chocolate.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: And a glass of tepid water.
lines.stage_direction: She gathers up all of these things.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ooh, is this one of those things where you throw it in a bag with some graveyard dirt and hang it from a north-facing tree?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh, only if you have the hiccups too. Yeah, the pictures are for you, the water and the chocolate is for me. I just didn’t feel like getting up. Okay, I’m gonna show you a picture of Ross. Okay? And you’re going to remember all of the bad things about him. All right? Really focus on his flaws.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I can do that. I certainly did it when we were going out.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. Umm, before we get started, I just wanna say for the record that I love Ross, I think he’s such a great guy. Here. Okay, now, close your eyes. And imagine that you’re with Ross okay and imagine that you’re kissing him. And you’re-you’re running your hands all over his body. And then you run your hands through his hair, but eew-oh gross it’s some kind of grease, it’s—uck! Hah?
lines.stage_direction: Hands her the picture, Rachel grabs it out of her hand.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don’t know, his uh, his hair never really bothered me that much, and y’know it was always more crunchy than it was greasy.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, this is going to be harder than I thought. Umm, let’s try some uh, aversion therapy.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So uh, now look at the picture…

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right, and umm…
lines.stage_direction: She grabs the picture and smacks her in the head.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ow!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, how do you feel now?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I like you less!

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's, Phoebe is on the couch as Rachel returns carrying a bunch of shopping bags.

scenes.location: Chandler and Joey’s hotel room. Chandler is watching TV as Joey returns

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh. Sorry!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No-no-no, y’know what? I really shouldn’t have said that you were embarrassing me, I mean that really wasn’t cool. And if it makes you feel any better, I’ve had a really lousy day.

lines.text: Me too.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Noo!! I’ve had the best day ever! Dude, check this out!

lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Sh-shh-shh!
lines.stage_direction: He motions for her to watch something he has taped.

lines.text: Okay, so say hi to my friend and tell him that you like the hat.

lines.speaker: Fergie
lines.text: Okay, so umm, what’s your friend’s name?
lines.stage_direction: Yep, Sarah, the Duchess of York

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, Chandler.

lines.text: Hi Chandler!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That’s… That’s was…

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That’s Fergie baby!!

lines.speaker: Fergie
lines.text: Joey says you don’t really like his hat, but I think it’s kinda dashing.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: How did you? How? How?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, I was trying to figure out how to get to Buckingham Palace, right? So, I’m in my map and-and… Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi. I understand you had a little talk with Emily.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes, I did! And you are welcome!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Am I?! And was it your idea to postpone the wedding?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Umm…

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I’m gonna go to the bathroom.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wait up!
lines.stage_direction: They both run to the bathroom.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey-hey, since you’re the ‘fix-it’ lady, here’s a pickle, what do you do when the bride says she doesn’t want to have the wedding at all?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: She said that?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Why?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don’t know, I told her it was stupid to put off the wedding just because the hall was gone and she liked flipped out.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God. You’re even dumber than I am!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Excuse me?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ross, how long have you been planning this wedding?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don’t know. A month?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Emily has probably been planning it since she was five! Ever since the first time she took a pillowcase and hung it off the back of her head. That’s what we did! We dreamed about the perfect wedding, and the perfect place, with the perfect four-tiered wedding cake , with the little people on top. Thanks. But the most important part is that we had the perfect guy who understood just how important all that other stuff was.
lines.stage_direction: Starting to cry Ross gets thrown a box of Kleenex from the bathroom and he gives her one.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I had no idea. And that-that pillowcase thing, I thought you guys were just doing the flying nun.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Sometimes we were.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come on. You gotta help me figure out what to do. Okay?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come on.
lines.stage_direction: They leave and Chandler sticks his head out.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That was pretty intense huh?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah. Hey, I hope Ross didn’t think that we just went in there because we were uncomfortable being out here!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I hope he did!

scenes.scene_description: Chandler and Joey’s hotel room. Chandler is watching TV as Joey returns, still wearing his hat.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, Pheebs! It’s Joey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey, Joey! Hey! Ooh! Ooh! I just say someone on the—that looks just like you on the subway. And I was gonna go over and say ‘hi!’ but then I figured, he doesn’t care if he looks like you.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That just cost me four bucks. But uh listen, I just called to see how the chick and the duck are doing?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ohh, they’re having a great time with their Aunt Phoebe! Aunt Rachel hasn’t been helpful at all. So, do you miss me?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Kinda, but I’ve just been having way too much fun.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So you’re not homesick yet?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, I don’t think so.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right, the seven of us miss you.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Who’s seven?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Y’know, me, Rachel, the birds, the babies…

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ahh, the babies miss me?

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's, the phone rings and Phoebe answers it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh, the pizza guy’s here!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What? You ordered pizza without me?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah. But y’know we were thinking about you, y’know we ordered the Joey Special.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Two pizzas?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yep! Okay, gotta go, talk to you later.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wait, well, where did you get it from?!

scenes.scene_description: There is a knock on Rachel and Phoebe’s door.

Phoebe has already hung up, leaving Joey in the dark. So Joey decides to watch some TV and turns on a rerun of Cheers , with the theme song playing. At first, he’s happy, but as the song progresses Joey gets depressed and homesick.

scenes.location: Ross and Emily’s planned wedding place

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Monica, why have you brought me here of all places?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You’ll see.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: I tell you, this wedding is not going to happen.

scenes.scene_description: Ross and Emily’s planned wedding place, Monica is dragging Emily in.

scenes.location: Ross and Emily’s planned wedding place

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Oh God.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay? But-but imagine a lot more lights, okay? And-and y’know fewer bricks, and-and-and flowers, and candles…

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: And the musicians, look, they can go over here , okay? And the chairs can face this way , and… You go.
lines.stage_direction: Points to a little alcove Points Points to Ross

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: But-but, if you don’t love this, we’ll do it in any other place at any other time. Really, it’s fine, whatever you want.

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: It’s perfect.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And, I don’t know, if it starts to rain…

lines.speaker: Emily
lines.text: Well then we’ll get wet.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ohh. And I don’t even have a date.

scenes.scene_description: At that Ross plugs in some Christmas lights to light the place up.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Pheebs?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Do you remember where the duck food is?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, it’s in the guys’ apartment under the sink. Why?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Because I’m going to London.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?! What do you mean you’re going to London?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, I have to tell Ross that I love him. Now honey, you take care, you don’t have those babies until I get back.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I—Rachel, you can’t go! Ross loves Emily!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, I know, I know, I know he does. But I have to tell him how I feel! He deserves to have all the information and then he can make an informed decision.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That’s not why you’re going! You’re going because you hope he’s gonna say, "Yeah, I love you too, Rach. Forget that British chippy."

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh—Do you think he will?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No! Because he’s in love with the British chippy! Look, Rachel, if you go, you’re just gonna mess with his head and ruin his wedding! Y’know, it’s too late! You missed you’re chance! I’m sorry, I know this must be really hard, it’s over.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Y’know what? No. It’s not over until someone says, "I do."

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I do! I do! I do! I do! Ugh, like I can really chase you. I’m carrying a litter.
lines.stage_direction: Chases her into the hall, but Rachel doesn’t stop. Gives up.

scenes.scene_description: Monica and Rachel's, Rachel is in her bedroom.

scenes.location: Moving Shot towards The Waltham House. A phone is ringing

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh...yes..is this..umm..Emily’s Parents’ house.

lines.speaker: Housekeeper
lines.text: Miss Waltham, is at the rehearsal dinner and it’s not polite to make fun of people. Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Joey, it’s been three days, okay.. Your just a little homesick, Okay. Would you just try to relax. Just, just try to enjoy yourself.

scenes.scene_description: The Girls apartment. Phoebe is dialing the phone and Rachel runs in the door.
